category_title: Timid students who feel voiceless and isolated in peer work
minimal_context: Brandon is assigned to work with a partner on a science activity in his 6th-grade classroom.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Brandon, it is time for partner work. Please discuss the science questions with your partner.

STUDENT: (Remains a quiet student and does not initiate the interaction.)

TUTOR: Are you two working on the questions together?

STUDENT: (Acts very timid and looks down at the desk.)

TUTOR: You need to share your thoughts with your partner to finish the assignment.

STUDENT: I feel like I have no voice... my partner doesn’t like to talk to me.

options.A: Explicitly identify the student as a professional and expert during science classroom partner work activities.
options.B: Subdivide science lessons into 15-minute segments and introduce short breaks between partner learning activities.
options.C: Implement a progressive disciplinary sequence during partner work: first a warning, then a 10-minute timeout, followed by classroom suspension or detention.
options.D: Use a two-pocket folder where the left side is dedicated to daily science work to be completed and the right to completed work.
